creator: marcus@FreeBSD.org
text:
Interesting.  I'm not seeing this, though, on my 14-STABLE machine.  When I run portlint -C on devel/raknet, I see:

WARN: Makefile: [55]: possible use of "${FIND} ... ${XARGS} ${RM}" when "${FIND} ... -delete" will work.
WARN: Makefile: [34]: Setting a specific version for USE_GCC should only be done as a last resort.  Unless you have confirmed this port does not build with later versions of GCC, please use USE_GCC=11+.
WARN: Makefile: "USES" has to appear earlier.
WARN: /usr/ports/devel/raknet/files/patch-CMakeLists.txt: patch was not generated using ``make makepatch''.  It is recommended to use ``make makepatch'' when you need to [re-]generate a patch to ensure proper patch format.
WARN: /usr/ports/devel/raknet/files/patch-Samples-Tests-PacketAndLowLevelTestsTest.cpp: patch was not generated using ``make makepatch''.  It is recommended to use ``make makepatch'' when you need to [re-]generate a patch to ensure proper patch format.
WARN: /usr/ports/devel/raknet/files/patch-Source-DS_BinarySearchTree.h: patch was not generated using ``make makepatch''.  It is recommended to use ``make makepatch'' when you need to [re-]generate a patch to ensure proper patch format.
WARN: /usr/ports/devel/raknet/files/patch-Source-RakPeer.cpp: patch was not generated using ``make makepatch''.  It is recommended to use ``make makepatch'' when you need to [re-]generate a patch to ensure proper patch format.
WARN: Consider to set DEVELOPER=yes in /etc/make.conf
0 fatal errors and 8 warnings found.
